title: Smarter, but Not Safer: An Empirical Analysis of the Functional-Security Gap in Evolving LLMs
data:
A Cannavale, G Recupito, G Catolino, C De Roover- International Conference on, 2026
Large Language Models (LLMs) have become tools for software development and 
maintenance, demonstrating impressive capabilities in automating tasks such as 
code generation and refactoring. However, it remains empirically unclear whether 
the rapid scaling of these capabilities is accompanied by proportional improvements 
in security, or whether a discrepancy, defined as the SecurityFunctionality Gap, 
persists across model generations. To address this question, we conducted an

title: Evaluating Large Language Models for Automated Code Vulnerability Detection
data:
A Ghorbel, O Hosam, N Tinkicht, GB Ayed- Journal of Information & Knowledge, 2026

classification using the DiverseVul benchmark: StarCoder2-7B, Phi-3.5-Mini-Instruct,
